page: Microsoft Azure F8als_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Falsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache per core, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_NI. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores in the strong (top 25%) category. Text processing tasks also perform in the top tier. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present distinct tradeoffs. While cached small-block read memory bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25% at 37.09 GB/sec. Large-block write memory bandwidth and multi-core stress-ng performance remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F8als_v6 offers a high-density compute profile with dedicated CPU resources, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The lack of bundled local storage and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represent a clear tradeoff, optimizing the instance for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive databases. Recommended workloads include single-threaded applications, text processing, lightweight software compilation, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F8als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance from the Falsv6 family, powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-core Geekbench scores. However, uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is weak, representing a notable tradeoff. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for dedicated, CPU-bound tasks that require high clock speeds but minimal memory. It is well-suited for workloads such as text processing, single-threaded applications, lightweight software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8als_v6 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 16 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suitable for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8als_v6 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized server with an AMD EPYC CPU, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated high-frequency AMD EPYC cores and a low memory footprint for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
Low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core (16.0 GB total RAM)
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Weak uncached large-block read memory bandwidth tradeoff
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and static web serving
